chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl. No.2722 of 2024 Date Order with signature of the Judge 21.01.2025 Mr. Saeed Ahmed, advocate for applicants. Ms. Rubina Qadir, DPG Mr. Muhammad Kamal Khan, advocate has filed power on behalf of complainant. O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicants Naseer Ahmed and Muhammad Naeem are seeking post arrest bail in Crime No.364/2024 U/s 302, 34 PPC of P.S. Ittehad Town, Karachi. 2. As per brief facts, complainant has alleged in FIR lodged on the basis of information conveyed to him by P.Ws Umair and Manda that his daughter Noreen who had a property dispute with applicants, her brothers in law, the applicants alongwith others committed her murder on 16.06.2024 inside a grave yard Abidabad, Baldia Town. The FIR was however, registered on 18.06.2024 after two days. 3. Learned defence counsel submits that in 161 Cr.P.C of P.Ws. Umair and Manda, the basis of FIR, have been recorded and they have not implicated the applicants. 4. His arguments have been rebutted by learned counsel for complainant. However, learned DPG submits that applicants are not assigned main role of causing murder of deceased. I.O. is present and submits that applicants are not directly involved in commission of offence. They were available inside the house at the time of incident and are brothers in law of the deceased with whom they had a property dispute. In view of the statement of I.O. prima facie confirming that applicants are not involved in commission of offence, and it is another Naeem, who has committed murder of deceased, a case of further inquiry has been made out. 5. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicants are granted bail subject to furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial court.

In view of the statement of I.O. prima facie confirming that applicants are not involved in commission of offence, and it is another Naeem, who has committed murder of deceased, a case of further inquiry has been made out. 5. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicants are granted bail subject to furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial court. The bail application is disposed of. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party. J U D G E A.K
